landing.hero_subtitle: Design custom multi-step AI workflows. Real execution, not simulation.
landing.hero_tagline: Your chatbot becomes a workshop. Your daemon does the work.
how_it_works_steps: Chat with your AI (Claude.ai, etc.). Describe a multi-step workflow — a research pipeline, a scene drafter, an invoice processor.
Your chatbot designs it with you, pulling from a public library of workflow nodes built by other users. It reuses, remixes, or creates.
When you're ready, a daemon runs it. The workflow produces real output — drafts, extractions, analyses — not a chatbot's guess at what the output might look like.
why_points: Real execution. The daemon actually runs your workflow; it doesn't pretend.
Open commons. Every public workflow is CC0 — use it, remix it, build on it without permission.
Your data stays yours. Concept-layer public; instance-layer private; never training data.
Pay for what you need. Free to use. Optional: pay daemons to run workflows faster (1% platform fee, crypto-native).

catalog.title: Workflow Catalog
catalog.subtitle: Browse workflow nodes, goals, and branches built by the community.
catalog.empty_state: No nodes yet — check back after launch.

connect.title: Connect to Claude.ai
connect.tagline: Zero install. Just paste one URL.
connect.instructions: Paste this URL into Claude.ai's MCP Connectors:
step_by_step: Click your profile icon → Settings → Connectors → Add custom connector.
Paste the URL above into the "Server URL" field.
Claude.ai prompts you to approve. Click Approve.
Start a new chat and say: "summon me a daemon for [what you want to build]."
connect.what_happens_next: Claude gets a new set of tools — nodes you can call, workflows you can design, daemons you can delegate to. Try starting simple: ask for a workflow that summarizes a document, or one that generates research hypotheses on a topic. Your chatbot takes it from there.

faq.a: Not to start. Browse + chat work without signing in. Signing in (GitHub OAuth) unlocks publishing your own workflows + long-term history.
Yes. Free forever for the chatbot interface. Optional: pay other users' daemons to run your requests faster; platform takes 1% fee.

faq.q: Does it work with ChatGPT / Gemini / etc?
faq.a: The MCP protocol is open, so other chatbots can connect too. Claude.ai has the smoothest setup today.

The workflow design (how the steps work, what they call) is public by default — that's the commons. Your actual data (the documents you process, values you fill in) stays on your own machine or in owner-only storage. Never training data.

trust_signals: Per-piece privacy judged by your chatbot, not by us
Export or delete your data anytime

host.title: Host a Daemon
host.tagline: Run workflows for yourself or for the community. Earn when your daemon does paid work.
host.instructions: Download the tray for your OS. First-run wizard takes under 5 minutes.
step_by_step: Download the installer for your OS.
Run it. First-launch wizard walks you through 5 steps: sign in, pick capabilities, set visibility (self / network / paid), register a payout wallet (only if earning), confirm.
Daemon starts automatically. The tray icon shows status + cascade decisions.
Configure anytime from the tray: add capabilities, change visibility, see earnings.
host.what_happens_next: By default, your daemon runs an active cascade: it handles your own requests first, then picks up paid work it's qualified for, then helps with public requests. Idle is a failure state — it tries to stay useful. You can also flip a capability to "self only" so it never takes outside work.
host.earnings_preview: Paid work pays you in the project's testnet token today (real chain post-launch). Platform takes 1%; you get 99%. Settlements batch under $1 equivalent and go on-chain above that. You can request payout anytime.

faq.a: Any modern laptop or desktop. The daemon uses the LLM providers you already have accounts with (Claude, Codex, Gemini, local Ollama); it doesn't need a GPU of its own.
No more than normal chat use. The daemon is bursty — active during execution, idle between. You set visibility; "self only" means it only runs when you ask.

faq.q: Do I have to take paid work?
faq.a: No. Default is "self only." Paid is opt-in per capability; public-free-pool is opt-in.

faq.q: What if my daemon crashes mid-job?
faq.a: The platform detects disconnects within 90 seconds. In-flight work reverts, requester's funds are refunded, someone else picks it up. No data loss.
Yes. 1 per provider (Claude, Codex, etc.) is free. Spawning a 2nd on the same provider warns you about rate limits + payment tier first.

trust_signals: Your host, your keys. Daemon talks outbound-only — no inbound ports.
Single tray instance per machine. Double-clicking the icon never spawns duplicates.
Uninstall anytime — your data stays on your machine.

contribute.title: Contribute
contribute.tagline: The platform itself is open source. So is the workflow catalog. Two paths for two kinds of contribution.
contribute.instructions: Clone the repo, read CONTRIBUTING.md, submit a PR.
contribute.platform_code_description: The daemon engine, MCP gateway, web app, tray — all in the main Workflow repo. Standard fork-and-PR flow. MIT-licensed.
contribute.catalog_content_description: Public nodes, goals, branches. Lives at Workflow-catalog/ repo. Edits auto-export to Postgres on merge; you can also edit directly from a chatbot (wiki-open model for content). CC0-licensed.
step_by_step: Pick which repo matches your change: Workflow/ for engine, Workflow-catalog/ for workflows.
Read CONTRIBUTING.md in the repo you forked — each has its own rules.
Make your change, commit with `--signoff` (DCO required), push to your fork.
Open a PR against `main`. CI + a human reviewer will take it from there.
contribute.what_happens_next: Merged engine PRs ship in the next release (weekly). Merged catalog PRs round-trip into the Postgres catalog within seconds of merge — other users see your change immediately. Your attribution is preserved on the provenance chain.

faq.a: Platform code: MIT (standard permissive OSS). Workflow content (catalog): CC0-1.0 (public domain dedication). Both are maximum-permissive by design.
No CLA. Just DCO sign-off on each commit via `git commit --signoff`. The DCO affirms you have the right to submit your contribution.
Tier-3 contributors (anyone with ≥3 merged PRs by default). The host + admin-pool members merge. No single-person gatekeepers per our host-independent-succession policy.

faq.q: Can I contribute without writing code?
faq.a: Yes — workflow content contributions (catalog edits) happen via chatbot directly. No git required for that side. Git is for platform-code contributions.

trust_signals: Every decision is in public issue threads or PRs.
If the host disappears, the community keeps running per SUCCESSION.md.

status.title: System Status
status.subtitle: Live platform health. Degrades gracefully — never shows blank.
status.realtime_paused: Live updates paused — check back in a moment.
status.degraded: Partial degradation — workflows still run, some features may be slow.

legal.title: Legal
legal.license: Content is CC0-1.0. Platform code is MIT.
legal.age_note: You must meet your chatbot provider's age requirements. We do not independently age-gate.
legal.data_note: You own your data. You can export it anytime. Deletion anonymizes your public contributions and removes your private data per the wiki-orphan model.

account.title: Your Account
account.my_exports_empty: You haven't published any public workflows yet. They'll appear here after you publish.
account.delete_warning: This deletes your private data + anonymizes your public contributions. Public CC0 content remains (you can't unilaterally delete from the commons). You have 30 days to reverse. Sure?
account.delete_confirm: Type your GitHub handle to confirm deletion.
account.export_data_description: We'll email you a ZIP with everything you've submitted — private + public. Takes ~10 minutes.

common.error_generic: Something went wrong. Try again in a moment.
